content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

The amendment identifies a real coupling between the custom Deserialize impl and serde_json::Value. The fix proposed (adding a doc comment) is minimal, zero-risk, and correctly scoped. I agree with the planner position to accept.

## Votes

- `fr-8-93dc626c`: Accept
  The observation is technically accurate: lines 163 and 169 use serde_json::Value::deserialize() and serde_json::from_value() respectively, which means the Deserialize impl will produce confusing runtime errors if used with non-JSON serde formats. While the type is currently only consumed in JSON contexts (LLM output parsing, NDJSON journals), the proposed fix — adding a doc comment documenting the JSON-only constraint — is essentially zero cost and zero risk. It protects future maintainers from a subtle trap where the type derives Deserialize (suggesting format-agnostic compatibility) but actually requires JSON internally. A single doc comment is the right level of intervention here; changing to serde_value::Value would be over-engineering for the current use case.
